results.student_answer: The Storm Prediction Center began monitoring the imminent threat that included this tornado event on May 3, 2020, at 12:17 PM CDT. They issued a moderate risk for severe weather, highlighting the potential for strong tornadoes in the area, which eventually led to the formation and path of the significant tornado that impacted the communities in Mississippi.

results.question: When did the Kentwood–Carson tornado occur and what were its start and end times?
results.reference_answer: Saturday, March 15, 2025; it began at 12:17 p.m. CDT and dissipated at 1:39 p.m. CDT.
results.student_answer: The Kentwood–Carson tornado occurred on May 22, 2020, from 12:17 p.m. CDT (UTC-05:00) to approximately 3:30 p.m. CDT.

results.question: Where did the Kentwood–Carson tornado begin?
results.reference_answer: One mile east-northeast of Kentwood in Tangipahoa Parish, Louisiana.
results.student_answer: The tornado began in Tangipahoa Parish, Louisiana, just off of LA 38 and to the east of I-55, one mile to the east-northeast of Kentwood in Tangipahoa Parish.

results.question: How many people did the EF4 Kentwood–Carson tornado kill?
results.student_answer: The EF4 Kentwood–Carson tornado did not result in any fatalities.

results.question: What maximum wind speed and Enhanced Fujita rating did the National Weather Service assign to the tornado?
results.reference_answer: Maximum winds of 170 mph (270 km/h), rated a low-end EF4.
results.student_answer: The National Weather Service assigned a maximum wind speed of 170 mph (273 km/h) and an Enhanced Fujita (EF) rating of EF4 to the tornado.

results.question: What damage did the tornado cause to the Paradise Ranch RV resort in Walthall County?
results.reference_answer: It destroyed all 27 cabins and 210 campsites.
results.student_answer: The tornado caused significant damage to the Paradise Ranch RV resort in Walthall County, including the destruction of multiple RVs and the collapse of a large portion of the roof of the main lodge.
